sections.paragraph:
εὔχρηστος, ον (s. χηρηστός; Hippocr. et al.; ins, pap, LXX; EpArist 136; Joseph.; Mel., P. 94, 723) pert. to being helpful or beneficial, useful, serviceable (a common term in Gr-Rom. lit. in description of service that has special social value), abs. (opp. ἄχρηστος) Hv 3, 6, 7. Of gold Hv 4, 3, 4. τινί to or for someone or someth.: w. dat. of pers. (PPetr III, 53 [n], 5) σκεῦος εὔ. τ. δεσπότῃ a vessel that is useful to the master 2 Ti 2:21. W. a play on the name Onesimus (opp. ἄχρηστος) σοὶ κ. ἐμοὶ εὔ. Phlm 11.—Hv 3, 6, 6b; m 5, 1, 6; Hs 9, 26, 4. Of members εὔ. ὅλῳ τ. σώματι 1 Cl 37:5. εὔ. γίνεσθαι w. dat. of pers. (IPriene 102, 5 [c. 100 b.c.] προγόνων γεγενημένων εὐχρήστων τῷ δήμῳ) Hv 3, 6, 6c; 7b; m 5, 1, 5; Hs 9, 15, 6. εὔ. εἴς τι for someth. (Diod S 5, 40, 1; IGR IV, 818, 23 εἰς χρίας κυριακὰς εὔχρηστον γενόμενον; Wsd 13:13; Jos., Ant. 4, 281) 2 Ti 4:11; Hv 3, 5, 5; 3, 6, 1; 6a.—DELG s.v. χρή. Larfeld I 497. M-M.

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Gr-Rom. Gr-Rom. = Greco-Roman (gener. in contrast to Israelite/Christian tradition)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

abs. abs. = absolute

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Hv Hv = Visions

someth. someth. = something

dat. dat. = dative

pers. pers. = person(s)

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

m m = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

IPriene IPriene = Die Inschriften von Priene—List 3

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

IGR IGR = Inscriptiones Graecae ad res Romanas pertinentes—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Larfeld Larfeld = WL., Handbuch der griechischen Epigraphik—Lists 3, 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 417
